HospitalRecommendations.Name: Medanta - The Medicity
HospitalRecommendations.Specialization: Medanta has a world-renowned Institute of Musculoskeletal Disorders and Orthopaedics. They utilize advanced techniques like computer-navigated surgery for knee replacements, ensuring high precision and faster recovery. Their international patient services are comprehensive.
HospitalRecommendations.ApproxTreatmentCost: $6,000 - $8,000 USD (for single knee replacement, includes standard tests, surgeon fees, and hospital stay)

HospitalRecommendations.Name: Fortis Memorial Research Institute (FMRI)
HospitalRecommendations.Specialization: The Fortis Bone & Joint Institute is a center of excellence for orthopedic procedures. They have a team of highly experienced surgeons and a dedicated physiotherapy department crucial for post-operative recovery. They are well-versed in handling international patients.

HospitalRecommendations.Name: Indraprastha Apollo Hospitals
HospitalRecommendations.Specialization: A pioneer in Indian private healthcare, the Apollo Institutes of Orthopaedics has one of the highest success rates for knee replacement surgeries in the region. Their comprehensive care includes pre-arrival assistance, airport transfers, and post-discharge follow-ups.

Itinerary.Notes: Arrive at Indira Gandhi International Airport. A pre-arranged hospital/hotel representative will meet you. Transfer to your pre-booked serviced apartment or hotel in Gurugram. Rest and acclimatize.
Take it easy to recover from jet lag. Your hospital coordinator can assist with local SIM cards or any other necessities.
Visit the hospital for your consultation with the orthopedic surgeon. Complete all pre-operative tests (blood work, X-rays, ECG, anesthesia clearance).
Get admitted to the hospital in the morning. The knee replacement surgery will be performed. Your wife can stay in the private room with you or at the nearby accommodation.
Post-operative care, pain management, and initial physiotherapy sessions. You will be encouraged to stand and walk with a walker under supervision.
Discharge to your apartment/hotel. The hospital will provide all necessary medications and a detailed recovery plan. A physiotherapist will be scheduled for home visits.
Focus on rest and daily physiotherapy. Minimal movement is advised initially. Towards the end of this period, short, wheelchair-assisted outings to nearby accessible places are possible.
Visit your surgeon for a follow-up appointment. Stitches or staples will be removed. Your progress will be assessed.
Continue with physiotherapy. You should feel a significant improvement in mobility, likely using crutches or a cane.
Final consultation with your surgeon to get a 'Fit to Travel' certificate. Begin your 7-day sightseeing tour with a private, comfortable car and driver. Drive to Agra (approx. 4 hours).
Visit the Taj Mahal (wheelchair accessible) in the morning. Later, visit Agra Fort, focusing on the accessible ground-level areas. Do not overexert.
Drive to Jaipur, the 'Pink City' (approx. 5 hours). Check into your hotel and rest for the evening.
Enjoy a comfortable city tour. Drive past Hawa Mahal (Palace of Winds), and visit the City Palace and Jantar Mantar (observatory), which are largely accessible on the ground floor.

Explore local markets like Bapu Bazaar for textiles and souvenirs (your wife can do the walking while you relax at a nearby cafe). In the afternoon, drive back to your accommodation in Delhi/Gurugram.
Rest from your trip. For a light outing, visit Dilli Haat, an accessible open-air craft bazaar, or select city temples that have ramp access. Final souvenir shopping.
Transfer to Delhi's IGI Airport (DEL) for your flight back to the United States. Ensure you have your medical documents and 'Fit to Fly' certificate handy.

RecoveryAndSightseeing.WhyRecommended: An upscale, open-air food and entertainment complex. It is completely accessible with ramps and elevators, perfect for a wheelchair or crutch-assisted outing. It's a great place to enjoy an evening without much walking.
RecoveryAndSightseeing.NearbyDietaryOptions: Excellent options for international cuisine (American, Italian, Mexican). High-end restaurants here can easily accommodate requests for low-sodium meals suitable for someone on blood pressure medication. No dietary restrictions will be an issue.

RecoveryAndSightseeing.WhyRecommended: A large, modern, and fully air-conditioned mall. It's wheelchair-friendly and offers a comfortable environment to spend a few hours, shop, or watch a movie. It provides a change of scenery from the hotel room without physical strain.
RecoveryAndSightseeing.NearbyDietaryOptions: The mall has a vast food court and numerous fine dining restaurants with diverse menus. Finding American or Continental food is very easy. All major food chains are present.

RecoveryAndSightseeing.WhyRecommended: Offers a spectacular, seated Bollywood-style musical show. The main theatre ('Nautanki Mahal') is accessible. It's a perfect cultural experience that is entertaining and requires no physical exertion.
RecoveryAndSightseeing.NearbyDietaryOptions: Inside, there is a food court called 'Culture Gully' representing various Indian states, but also offering general snacks and beverages. It's best to eat at a more controlled restaurant in Cyber Hub before or after the show for specific dietary needs.

RecoveryAndSightseeing.WhyRecommended: It's important to manage your diet post-surgery, especially with your blood pressure condition. All recommended JCI-accredited hospitals have dedicated dieticians who will create a meal plan for you based on your medical needs and preference for American/Continental food. Most upscale hotels and serviced apartments in Gurugram offer 24/7 room service with international menus and can cater to low-sodium and other specific dietary requests.
RecoveryAndSightseeing.NearbyDietaryOptions: Your wife can also use services like 'Zomato' or 'Swiggy' (Indian food delivery apps) to order from a wide variety of vetted, high-quality restaurants directly to your accommodation.
